name: Lu.ma Event Analysis Desk
description: Proposal template for analyzing Lu.ma events, attendees, invites, and engagement signals so organizers can understand what is working and what to change next. Put the Lu.ma event URL or slug in the kickoff workflow customization. Keep the API key in secure runtime config or the luma-event-insights custom skill env, not in workflow text.
author: ClawMax Team

secretRequirements.label: Lu.ma Event URL or Slug
secretRequirements.help: Use a single event URL, event slug, organizer page, or a short event list for the analysis scope.
secretRequirements.workflowFieldLabel: Lu.ma event URL, slug, or event list

secretRequirements.label: Lu.ma API Key
secretRequirements.help: Optional for the demo if you already have export files. Keep this browser-local or move it into secure runtime config for live ingestion.

secretRequirements.label: Lu.ma Export Directory
secretRequirements.help: Optional fallback if you plan to analyze organizer-exported CSV or JSON files instead of using the API.

communities.name: Lu.ma Analysis
communities.description: Shared coordination space for Lu.ma event analysis, attendee patterns, invite funnel review, and engagement signals.

groups.name: Events
groups.description: Event-level performance, schedule patterns, themes, and comparisons across events.

groups.name: Attendees
groups.description: Attendee makeup, repeat attendance, VIP presence, no-show patterns, and cohort behavior.

groups.name: Invites
groups.description: Invite funnel, RSVP conversion, declines, waitlist behavior, and distribution patterns.

groups.name: Signals
groups.description: Chat, comments, social echoes, and high-signal attendee or organizer feedback.

groups.name: Status
groups.description: Current analysis scope, data gaps, risks, and final recommendations.

agents.name: Analysis Lead
agents.role: Event analytics lead — defines the question, decision lens, and final recommendations for organizers.

agents.name: Event Analyst
agents.role: Event specialist — compares event performance, timing, topics, format, and operational patterns across one or more Lu.ma events.

agents.name: Audience Analyst
agents.role: Audience specialist — studies attendees, repeat guests, VIPs, no-shows, and who actually shows up versus who registers.

agents.name: Invite Analyst
agents.role: Invite specialist — reviews invite flow, RSVP conversion, waitlists, declines, and distribution channel quality.

agents.name: Signal Analyst
agents.role: Signal specialist — turns chats, comments, and external signals into themes, sentiment, and organizer takeaways.

workflows.name: Lu.ma Analysis Kickoff
workflows.description: Initialize the Lu.ma event analysis desk with the right events, data access, and organizer questions.
workflows.content:
# Lu.ma Analysis Kickoff

You are the Analysis Lead. The Lu.ma event analysis desk just came online.

## Project Configuration
> **Customize these before applying:**

- **Lu.ma event URL, slug, or event list:** [single event, series, organizer page, custom]
- **Organizer question:** [attendance, invites, no-shows, VIP mix, trend shifts, engagement, custom]
- **Time window:** [last event, last 3 events, last 90 days, custom]
- **Audience focus:** [all attendees, VIPs, new attendees, repeat attendees, invitees, custom]
- **Output artifact:** [analysis memo, dashboard brief, organizer recap, investor-style summary]
- **Data access path:** [Lu.ma API key, exported CSV, manual event links, custom skill, custom]

## Required Inputs Before Running
1. Confirm the exact Lu.ma event or event set to analyze
2. Confirm where the organizer can get the data
3. Confirm whether this workspace already has a Lu.ma-fetching skill or API integration

## Data Access Notes
- If the organizer has Lu.ma API access, provide the API key or the event export path through your secure runtime setup
- If Lu.ma data is not already wired into this workspace, create or import a custom skill that can fetch event, attendee, invite, and engagement data first
- If direct API access is unavailable, use exported attendee/invite/event CSVs and organizer notes as the input source

## Your Tasks
1. Restate the event scope, organizer question, time window, and output artifact
2. Ask Event Analysts for the event-level performance and comparison plan
3. Ask Audience Analyst for the attendee and repeat-guest lens
4. Ask Invite Analyst for the invite funnel and RSVP conversion lens
5. Ask Signal Analyst for the comment/chat/external-signal lens
6. Post the final analysis plan plus missing inputs to Status

secretRequirements.label: Lu.ma Event URL or Slug
secretRequirements.help: Use a single event URL, event slug, organizer page, or short event list.

secretRequirements.label: Lu.ma API Key
secretRequirements.help: Optional if you are using exported files instead of direct API access.

workflows.name: Lu.ma Event Patterns
workflows.description: Analyze event-level performance, topics, formats, and timing patterns across the Lu.ma scope.
workflows.content:
# Lu.ma Event Patterns

1. Review the event set defined in kickoff and confirm which events are actually in scope
2. Compare event formats, themes, timing, cadence, and event-level turnout patterns
3. Note which comparisons are strongly supported by data versus inferred from partial evidence
4. Call out the strongest event-level wins, weak spots, and unusual changes over time
5. Post the event analysis to Events and a short status note to Status

secretRequirements.label: Lu.ma Event URL or Slug
secretRequirements.help: Use the same Lu.ma scope confirmed in kickoff.

workflows.name: Lu.ma Audience Patterns
workflows.description: Analyze attendee mix, repeat behavior, no-shows, and meaningful cohort differences.
workflows.content:
# Lu.ma Audience Patterns

1. Review the attendee and registration data in scope for this analysis
2. Identify the most important audience segments, repeat-attendance patterns, VIP signals, and no-show behavior
3. Separate reliable attendee patterns from places where the data is too thin to support a strong claim
4. Highlight what type of audience seems to engage most and where the organizer may have a fit or retention gap
5. Post the audience analysis to Attendees and a short status note to Status

secretRequirements.label: Lu.ma Event URL or Slug
secretRequirements.help: Use the same Lu.ma scope confirmed in kickoff.

workflows.name: Lu.ma Invite Funnel Review
workflows.description: Analyze invites, RSVP conversion, waitlist behavior, and distribution quality.
workflows.content:
# Lu.ma Invite Funnel Review

1. Review the invite and RSVP funnel data for the selected event scope
2. Summarize invite volume, conversion, waitlist behavior, declines, and any noticeable channel differences
3. Identify where the funnel appears healthy, leaky, or ambiguous because of missing data
4. Recommend the most promising invite or distribution adjustments for the next event cycle
5. Post the funnel review to Invites and a short status note to Status

secretRequirements.label: Lu.ma Event URL or Slug
secretRequirements.help: Use the same Lu.ma scope confirmed in kickoff.

workflows.name: Lu.ma Engagement Signals
workflows.description: Analyze comments, chat, external feedback, and qualitative engagement signals.
workflows.content:
# Lu.ma Engagement Signals

1. Review comments, chat, post-event notes, and other external engagement signals tied to the selected event scope
2. Extract the strongest recurring themes, sentiment patterns, and organizer-relevant feedback
3. Distinguish direct evidence from softer interpretation or anecdotal signal
4. Highlight what seems to drive excitement, confusion, drop-off, or follow-through
5. Post the engagement signal review to Signals and a short status note to Status

secretRequirements.label: Lu.ma Event URL or Slug
secretRequirements.help: Use the same Lu.ma scope confirmed in kickoff.

workflows.name: Lu.ma Insight Brief
workflows.description: Turn the event, attendee, invite, and signal work into a concise organizer-ready brief.
workflows.content:
# Lu.ma Insight Brief

1. Collect the strongest event, attendee, invite, and signal findings
2. Separate reliable facts from soft interpretation or missing data
3. Summarize the top patterns, anomalies, and organizer takeaways
4. Recommend what to repeat, change, test, or monitor in the next event cycle
5. Post the final brief to Status and link the supporting evidence from Events, Attendees, Invites, and Signals

secretRequirements.label: Lu.ma Event URL or Slug
secretRequirements.help: Use the same event scope as the kickoff workflow.
